body:
## Summary
- Route Progress store startup reads through the shared recoverable read/JSON parse helpers so malformed `progressState` hydrates empty state with a read warning instead of silently clearing the warning.
- Route Review store JSON parsing through `parseJsonRecoverably` so malformed `learning.reviews.cards.v1` produces the same recoverable read warning shape while preserving valid persisted data and write behavior.

## Verification
- `NODE_PATH=/home/billy/Swedish_Civic_Test/node_modules PATH=/home/billy/Swedish_Civic_Test/node_modules/.bin:$PATH NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' node --test --test-name-pattern "corrupt JSON reads" tests/content-storage-write-fail-soft.test.js`
- `NODE_PATH=/home/billy/Swedish_Civic_Test/node_modules PATH=/home/billy/Swedish_Civic_Test/node_modules/.bin:$PATH NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' node --test --test-name-pattern "successful writes persist JSON and corrupt reads|throwing MMKV reads" tests/v1-1-review-store.test.js`
- `NODE_PATH=/home/billy/Swedish_Civic_Test/node_modules PATH=/home/billy/Swedish_Civic_Test/node_modules/.bin:$PATH NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' node --test --test-name-pattern "progress hydration falls back when MMKV reads throw|progress corrupt JSON" tests/content-progress-schema-parity.test.js tests/content-storage-write-fail-soft.test.js`
- `npm run typecheck -- --pretty false`
- `git diff --check`

Note: the broader queue-provided pattern also selects unrelated current-main/open-task tests such as Settings route warning-source assertions, Review finite cap/runtime-id guards, and Highlight runtime input guards. The corrupt JSON/read-warning coverage above is the task-specific slice.
title: storage: warn on corrupt local study JSON
